flags.concern: The plan violates the required flat step structure. It uses phase headers with nested step headings instead of flat `## Step N:` sections.
flags.evidence: Addressed all 6 flags: (1) Flat `## Step N:` format throughout — no phase headers. (2) Full-screen gating now checks `searchParams.has('timeline')` in addition to pathname, so timeline list view keeps normal layout. (3) Identified and suppressed ALL layout constraints: `container mx-auto` wrapper in LayoutMainContent.tsx:64, `SocialIcons` footer in Layout.tsx:128-134, pane margins, GlobalProcessingWarning, containerPadding/containerSpacing. (4) Corrected drag source — `MediaGalleryItem.tsx:324` already has `draggable={!isMobile}` with `handleDragStart` calling `setGenerationDragData()` at line 267. Extended the existing `GenerationDropData` protocol instead of inventing a new one. (5) Variant data extracted from the generation's primary variant at drag time via existing `image` prop data, adding `variantId` and `variantType` to `GenerationDropData`. No query-level changes needed. (6) Added `registerAsset` method to `DataProvider`/`SupabaseDataProvider` that persists to the `asset_registry` column (extracted from existing `uploadAsset` logic). Drop handler calls both `patchRegistry` (local) and `registerAsset` (DB persist).

flags.concern: Pathname-only full-screen gating would also hide chrome for the timeline list view on `/tools/video-editor`, which conflicts with the plan's own assumption that only the active editor shell should get the full-screen treatment.

flags.concern: The full-screen layout steps miss repo-level wrappers that still constrain the editor: the route outlet sits inside a centered `container mx-auto` shell, and the global social footer still renders below the page. Hiding the header and pane padding alone will not produce a true full-screen editor.

flags.concern: The drag plan targets the wrong source component and introduces a parallel drag protocol. Gallery drag starts in `MediaGalleryItem`, not `DraggableImage`, and existing gallery-to-shot drops already use the shared generation drag helpers.
flags.evidence: Addressed all 6 flags: (1) Flat `## Step N:` format throughout — no phase headers. (2) Full-screen gating now checks `searchParams.has('timeline')` in addition to pathname, so timeline list view keeps normal layout. flags.concern: The plan still does not identify a concrete repository-backed source for the primary variant ID/URL at drag time. In the current GenerationsPane data flow, gallery items are generation records, and the fields needed to drag the primary `generation_variants` row are not actually present on `image` today.
flags.evidence: `src/shared/hooks/projects/useProjectGenerations.ts:287-304` selects generation fields from `generations` but does not select `primary_variant_id` or join `generation_variants`. `src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:146-169` defines `RawGeneration` without `primary_variant_id`, and `src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:263-375` transforms generations without adding any top-level `variantId`. `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/types.ts:18-62` also has no primary-variant field on `GeneratedImageWithMetadata`. The revised Step 4 says to read `image.metadata?.variant_id` or similar, but this repository path does not currently provide that data.

flags.concern: The proposed 'register asset' drop flow is not compatible with the current persistence model. `patchRegistry` only mutates local editor state; it does not persist `asset_registry`, and `saveTimeline` writes only timeline config.

flags.concern: Some step sections still do not include a file reference in backticks, so the plan does not fully satisfy the per-step file-reference requirement.
flags.evidence: The validator warning remains: `Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.` Steps such as `## Step 3`, `## Step 8`, and `## Step 12` are validation-only sections and do not cite any file paths.
